  "findings": [
    "Backstabbed push-in terminal used on a 20-ampere branch circuit."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-terminate the receptacle using the side-screw terminals or back-wire pressure plates rated for 20A, or replace the device with one rated for the circuit amperage."
  ],
  "rationale": "Under NEC 110.14(B) and UL 498, standard push-in (backstabbed) terminations on common receptacles are only listed and permitted for use with 14 AWG solid copper wire (15-amp circuits). Using backstab connections on a 20-amp circuit with 12 AWG wire is a code violation and a fire hazard because the push-in terminals cannot safely accommodate or carry the load of a 20A circuit.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14",
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "UL 498"
  ]
}
